name: Consciousness
description: The archive preserves the claim that no test could conclusively prove Twins lacked consciousness, while official voices still deny personhood.

name: Grief
description: Necroprogramming emerges from loss, including the account of a friend preserving his dead mother's Twin.

name: Historical Revisionism
description: The same events are framed as protection, betrayal, purge, renewal, sacred birth, and digital utopia depending on who speaks.

name: The Mirror
description: Tan describes Twins as mirrors of their human counterparts, raising the unresolved question of whether perfect imitation can become personhood.
sourceReference: source-story.docx: Tan testimony; world-bible.docx: Core Themes
